Home / Indian Marbles / Makrana Marbles

Makrana Marbles

Makrana Marble: A 98% Calcium Carbonate Wonder

Makrana marble is one of the oldest and most beautiful marble in the world. This Makrana White Marble marble is renowned for its pristine white color and luxurious appearance, making it a popular choice for flooring and in upscale homes and commercial spaces. The smooth texture and glossy finish of Makrana white marble tiles create a stunning visual impact, making them an ideal choice for bathrooms, kitchens, or even outdoor patios. The Makrana marble price reflects its high quality, as this exquisite natural stone is known for its durability and timeless elegance.

Known for its ethereal beauty and unparalleled purity of 98% calcium carbonate, this marvel of nature has embellished palaces, temples, and monuments for centuries, including the Taj Mahal and Victoria Memorial. The pure white colour of makrana marble and intricate veining patterns have made it a symbol of luxury and opulence.

Global Heritage Stone

According to the International Union of Geological Sciences (IUGS), marble from Makrana in Rajasthan is now Asia’s first Global Heritage Stone Resource (GHSR).

What is the Price of Makrana marble per square foot in India? 

The price of Makrana marble for flooring in India is Rs. 30-700 per square foot. This luxurious and high-quality marble is sourced from the famous quarries of Makrana, Rajasthan and is known for its stunning white colour and durability. In general, Makrana Marble Tiles cost around Rs 30 per square foot, making them an affordable option for anyone looking to add an air of luxury to their home or business. In contrast, as you increase in quality and rarity, the price can increase substantially, reaching upwards of Rs 150 or even Rs 250 per square foot for the most premium options.

Top Qualities of Makrana Marble

1. Makrana White Marble is resistant to water seepage because it contains 98 per cent calcium carbonate and 2 per cent impurities. No other chemical treatments are applied to these.

2. The antimicrobial properties of white marble make it ideal for use in areas susceptible to infection, such as the kitchen and bathroom.

3. It is rich in thermal mass, which absorbs and stores heat, keeping your home cool in the summer and warm in the winter.

Famous Structures Made from Makrana Marble

1. Taj Mahal- The Iconic Symbol of love
2. Victoria Memorial- A blend of Indian and British Architecture
3. Jain Temples at Dilwara- A Masterpiece of Intricate Carvings
4. Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque- A Modern Marvel In Abu Dhabi
5. Rambagh Palace Hotel Jaipur: A Perfect Masterpiece of Heritage
6. Moti Masjid: Lahore Fort, Pakistan.

Uses of Makrana Marble

1. Flooring and Wall Cladding
2. Countertops and Vanity Tops
3. Sculptures and Decorative Items
4. Architectural features
5. Landscaping
6. Furniture

Frequently Asked Questions

Got a question? We`re here to answer ! If you don`t see your question here, drop us a line on our Contact Page

The Cost of Makrana marble can vary depending on factors such as the grade of the marble, transportation costs, availability, and demand in the market. In India, Makrana marble costs typically range from Rs. 30-700 per square foot. Lower-grade varieties may be more affordable at around Rs. 30-250 per square foot, while premium grades with intricate veining patterns or rare pure white marble can fetch Rs. 700 per square foot.

While all types of Makrana marble are known for their beauty, durability, and quality, However, our experts highly recommend the Makrana Pure White marble and Albeta White Marble for its stunning white color and high quality. It has been used in iconic monuments such as the Taj Mahal and is known for its durability and timeless beauty. Although, we also offer other types such as Dungri and Kumari marble to cater to different preferences and budgets. Trust Makrana Marble for your next project.

Makrana marble is renowned for its purity and rareness, Its pure white colour, lack of impurities, and unique natural veining make it highly desirable among architects and interior designers. Some of India's most famous buildings were made from Makrana marble. Makrana marble was used in the Taj Mahal. Makrana marble is highly sought-after by locals and international buyers.

1. Firstly, look for its signature white colour which should be consistent throughout the slab. 2. Next, check for its translucent texture, which is a unique characteristic of Makrana Marble. 3. It should also be free from any cracks, chips, or stains, indicating its quality and durability. 4. Lastly, run your hand over the surface to feel the smoothness and check for a polished finish.

Makrana marble, renowned for its exceptional white appearance, is a top choice among homeowners and designers alike for flooring applications. With its smooth and glossy finish, this marble exudes elegance and sophistication in any space it adorns. Not only limited to flooring, Makrana marble is also commonly used for wall cladding, adding a touch of luxury to interiors.

Without a doubt, the most famous and highly sought after marble in Rajasthan is Makrana Marble. This luxurious and beautiful marble has been used for centuries by the Mughals and Rajputs to adorn their palaces, forts, and monuments. It is known for its purity, durability, and exquisite white color with subtle grey veins running through it. Makrana Marble is not just limited to Rajasthan but it has gained global recognition for its quality and beauty, making it the top choice of architects and interior designers all over the world. So if you're looking for a touch of royalty in your home or building project, there's no better option than Makrana Marble from Rajasthan.

Makrana marble is also commonly known as "Sangemarmar Marble”. So, whether you call it Makrana or Sangemarmar, you can be sure that you are getting the highest quality pure white marble with a timeless and elegant look for your home or project.

Yes, Makrana Marble is considered to be better than Italian Marble due to its superior quality and durability. It is a pure white marble with very fine grains, making it more aesthetically pleasing. Additionally, it has a high level of calcium content which makes it extremely strong and less prone to damage or staining. Makrana Marble also has a lower water absorption rate compared to Italian Marble, making it more suitable for use in areas with high moisture levels.

There are a few key points to look out for when identifying genuine Makrana Marble. Firstly, check for the characteristic white color with subtle gray or beige veins running through it. The marble should also have a high level of translucency, meaning it allows light to pass through it. Secondly, make sure that the surface has a smooth and lustrous finish, with no signs of chips or scratches. Lastly, you can also do a simple acid test by applying lemon juice or vinegar on a small area of the marble - if it bubbles up, then you know it is authentic Makrana Marble as it contains high levels of calcium carbonate.

Makrana Marble is truly a masterpiece of nature. It is known for its stunning pure white color and unique smooth, crystalline texture which sets it apart from other types of marble. Unlike other marbles that may have veins or patterns, Makrana Marble is prized for its consistent appearance that adds a touch of elegance and sophistication to any space. Additionally, Makrana Marble has been used in the construction of iconic structures such as the Taj Mahal, showcasing its durability and timeless beauty.
